evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly conveys up/down/non-sig classification with threshold lines and top-5 annotations; brief's core claim is fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type 3 bitmapped glyphs (not editable TrueType/Type 42); default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Red/blue for up/down is directional and colorblind-distinguishable (deuteranopia-safe); grey for non-sig is appropriate; palette is mostly CB-safe but red/green not used, good practice.
evidence.layout_density: Panel fills the 6x5 figure well, no gaping whitespace; legend placed inside axes without overlap; y-axis range sensibly covers data; no label collisions visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes gene counts; legend inside plot could be questioned by reviewers; gene labels are index-based (acknowledged in caption); overall presentation is clean.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf uses Type 3 (bitmapped) fonts, not Type 42 (TrueType/editable); text is NOT selectable/editable; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (pdf=86 KB, png=92 KB, svg=132 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explicitly states n=235 up, n=230 down, n=2535 non-sig, describes axes, thresholds, and labeling rationale; specific numerical finding present.

overall_strengths: Clear scientific communication: volcano plot faithfully implements the brief with correct thresholds, 3-color classification, and top-5 annotations.
Strong caption with specific numerical claims (n=235 up, n=230 down, n=2535 non-sig) and complete description of axes and thresholds.
overall_weaknesses: Typography/vector fidelity failure: missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=0 means fonts are bitmapped Type 3 in PDF (not editable) and DejaVu Sans default is used instead of a publication-quality font.
PDF text is not selectable or editable by downstream editors, which is a standard requirement for journal submission.
